name: Uraeus Serpent
description: The Uraeus is an upright cobra, a symbol of sovereignty, royalty, deity, and divine authority in ancient Egypt. It represents the goddess Wadjet, one of the oldest Egyptian deities, often depicted as a cobra. The Uraeus was worn by pharaohs on their crowns and headdresses, signifying their divine right to rule and their protection by Wadjet. Its presence was believed to ward off enemies and ensure the pharaoh's safety. The Uraeus could also be associated with other powerful goddesses like Bastet, Sekhmet, and Mut, each embodying different aspects of divine power and protection. It symbolized the sun's fiery power and the ability to strike down enemies with swift and deadly force. The Uraeus was not merely a decorative element; it was a potent symbol imbued with deep religious and political significance, reinforcing the pharaoh's status as an intermediary between the gods and the people.
subtitle: Cobra of Divine Authority
appearance: The Uraeus is typically depicted as an erect cobra, poised to strike. It is often adorned with the sun disk, further emphasizing its connection to solar deities and royal power. The cobra's hood is flared, displaying its intimidating presence. It is usually crafted from gold or other precious materials when used as a royal ornament, signifying its value and importance.
habitat: The Uraeus is primarily a symbolic creature, residing on the crowns and headdresses of pharaohs and depicted in temples and tombs. While its physical counterpart, the cobra, inhabits various regions of Egypt, the Uraeus exists within the realm of royal power and divine representation.
behavior: The Uraeus is a guardian figure, fiercely protective of the pharaoh and the kingdom. It is always vigilant, ready to strike down any threat to the established order. While not typically aggressive unless provoked, its presence serves as a constant reminder of the pharaoh's power and the consequences of challenging their authority.

keyMyths.title: Wadjet as Protector
keyMyths.description: Wadjet, represented by the Uraeus, protected Horus as a baby from Set's machinations. This myth highlights the Uraeus's role as a divine protector of royalty and the rightful heir.

sources.author: Richard H. Wilkinson
